description: §26.8.1 / §26.5 — a class defined via `@apply` (`.card { @apply px-4 py-2; }`) is an author-defined class and draws NO W-TAILWIND-UNRECOGNIZED-CLASS wherever it is used, INCLUDING when applied dynamically (`class="${@active ? 'card' : ''}"`). The §26.5 unrecognized-class-lint deferral resolved by §26.8.
rationale: §26.8.1: the enclosing selector of an `@apply` rule (`.card`) is an author-defined class (§26.5), so it draws no W-TAILWIND-UNRECOGNIZED-CLASS wherever applied — INCLUDING when applied dynamically inside a `${...}` interpolation (`class="${@active ? 'card' : ''}"`), the exact dynamic form §26.5.1 discusses for utility tokens. The `@apply` composition is itself clean (no E-APPLY-*). This is the §26.5 author-class deferral resolved at §26.8: a named class an author composes is not a misspelled utility. (`@active` is also read in text so the cell is well-formed — orthogonal to the lint assertion.)
